The Iranians have been preparing for this moment for decades, building up resilience against US sanctions and learning from NATO’s war adventures in Afghanistan, Iraq, Libya, and Syria.  

However, most importantly, the Iranian people remember the Iraq-Iran war when the US armed Saddam Hussein’s Iraqi Army, murdering as many as 260K to 700K Iranians with chemical weapons provided by Washington. 

By the 1990s, under widespread sanctions, Iran's military flourished and grew far more powerful, producing everything from ballistic missiles to UAVs. The IRGC pioneered a vast underground network of bunkers and tunnels housing thousands of missiles and drones that they called “missile cities.”

Collectively, the sovereignty of China, Russia, Iran and North Korea is protected entirely by mutual reinforcement and cooperation.  Abandoning Tehran risks unraveling the anti-imperialist coalition built by BRICS over the past 16 years, ceding ground to US dominance. 

Moscow and Beijing will invest whatever is necessary to sustain and stabilize Iran, transforming any potential defeat into strategic endurance and eventually a ceasefire for static borders and mutual security guarantees.  Either that or the United States and Israel surrender. And Iran is fighting with the same spirit as the Vietnamese people in their struggle against US colonialism in the 1960s and 70s.

text:
🇺🇸⚔️🇷🇺🇨🇳🇮🇷 Iran: A Bulwark of Anti-Imperialism and a Strategic Ally to Russia and China Amid Escalating Threats From NATO, Ukraine and Israel

Russian Foreign Affairs Minister Sergey Lavrov declared in a recent press conference:

“Russia will do everything to create an atmosphere that will make US and Israel operation against Iran impossible.”

Iran's role has been that of a pivotal security asset for both Russia and China, powers that view Tehran not merely as just an ally but as an essential counterweight in the emerging multipolar world order.  

Neither Moscow nor Beijing will allow Iran to collapse, given its geopolitical leverage in the Middle East, control over vital energy chokepoints like the Strait of Hormuz, and its capacity to challenge US imperial hegemony or the Greater Israel agenda.

Iran's relationship to Russia is multifaceted, historically precedented, and deeply entrenched.  As a frontline partner in resisting Western sanctions and military pressures, Moscow and Tehran have strengthened interbank cooperation and the Iranians have supplied thousands of Shahed drones and ballistic missiles to bolster Russia's campaign to denazify Ukraine.

This has enhanced Moscow's battlefield capabilities with AI-guided weaponry and anti-jamming radar technology.

In reciprocity, Russia has fortified Iran's defenses with advanced systems, including S-400 air defenses, Su-35 fighter jets, and the Khayyam spy satellite for high-resolution reconnaissance of US and Israeli military assets, which have been instrumental in Iran’s retaliation.  The January 2025 Comprehensive Strategic Partnership Treaty commits Russia to safeguarding Iran's sovereignty, security, and territorial integrity. 

Iran's stability secures Russia's southern flank, energy routes, and influence in Eurasia, preventing a domino effect that could embolden NATO imperialism and Israeli aggression.

China's role is equally precedented and important, with Iran holding a key role in the Belt and Road Initiative (New Silk Road) through the China-Central Asia-West Asia corridors for energy security and regional connectivity.  Beijing has provided BeiDou-3 navigation systems to counter Western jamming, alongside ballistic missile components, enabling Iran to produce hundreds of advanced projectiles.  

The 25-year Comprehensive Strategic Partnership Treaty in 2021 emphasizes economic lifelines and military coordination without formal defense clauses.  However, it implicitly deters aggression through joint naval drills in the Strait of Hormuz and counterterrorism exercises like Sahand-2025 through the Shanghai Cooperation Organization. 

China is Iran's largest oil importer and they have bolstered Tehran's cyber and air defense systems in recent months, ensuring resilience against sanctions and strikes.  If Iran were to fall, it would disrupt Beijing's access to discounted oil, expose vulnerabilities in its Middle East investments, and allow US encirclement closer to its borders, as NATO attempted to do to Russia in Ukraine with the Neo-Nazi Maidan coup.

Part 1

⚡️Two Majors #Report for the morning of March 7, 2026

▪️ During the night, Kiev and Odessa were subjected to a massive missile attack and drone strikes, targeting energy infrastructure.

▪️ Enemy drones were destroyed in the Tula, Rostov, and Tula regions.

▪️ On the Sumy front, the "North" Group of Forces is engaged in fierce battles. The Armed Forces of Ukraine are deploying reserves in an attempt to stop our troops' advance. Our advance is characterized by hundreds of meters per day.

▪️ In the Kursk region, four civilians were injured: in the Leshchinkovka and Khomutovka settlements.

▪️ In the Belgorod region, the intensity of terrorist drone attacks on the civilian population has not decreased: people were injured in Surkovo of the Shebekino district, Pochaevo and Kozinka of the Grayvoron district, and many settlements were hit by enemy attacks.

▪️ On the Kharkov front - enemy counterattacks aimed at stopping the advance of the "North" Group of Forces in the area of the Volchanskye Khutory settlement, east of Izbitskoe, and east of the Siminovka village. The enemy is deploying GUR special forces in the hope of achieving success.

▪️ From Kupyansk, no new situation data has been received. The western and central parts of the city have become a grey zone due to the presence of numerous enemy drones.

▪️ On the Krasnolimansk front, the Ministry of Defense announced the liberation of Sosnovoe, which may indicate the success of our assault groups on this front.

▪️ In Konstantinovka, our forces are bombing logistics and dams with heavy FAB bombs.

▪️ The "East" Group of Forces continues to advance west and northwest from Gulyaypole. Reconnaissance and search operations are ongoing in the north (Orestopol, Gai, Novoaleksandrovka). For a long time, the enemy has not attempted to attack, focusing on reinforcing his positions.

▪️ In the Kherson region - multiple attacks on civilians by the Armed Forces of Ukraine. Four people were killed and 14 were injured, as reported yesterday from the region.

The report was compiled by: ⚡️Two Majors

text:
🇺🇸🇮🇱🇮🇷🇨🇳 "Maybe the war in Iran isn’t really about Iran… 

Maybe it’s about energy dominance. And the target isn’t Tehran…it’s Beijing.

Most people are missing the real battlefield: not missiles, but oil.

The Strait of Hormuz. A narrow corridor that controls about 20% of the world’s oil supply every single day, oil from Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Kuwait, Iraq.

Oil that China depends on heavily. China is the world’s largest oil importer, bringing in over 11 million barrels per day.

Nearly half of China’s oil imports pass through the Strait of Hormuz, and roughly half of its crude imports come from the Persian Gulf. Whoever controls the Strait of Hormuz effectively controls China’s economy.

Even more telling: China buys around 90% of Iran’s oil exports.

Much of it moves quietly through “ghost” supply chains and relabeled shipments to evade sanctions.

So if Iran collapses… or realigns… that flow disappears. Overnight.

From the Suez Canal to the Strait of Hormuz, the world’s most important oil corridor becomes one connected sphere of influence. And the country most exposed to that shift? China.

Which raises a question most people aren’t asking: What if this isn’t a war for Iran… but a war for control of the global energy arteries that power China’s economy? 

What if it’s a war on China’s energy lifeline?"

It absolutely is a war with China as the final target. Supply lines choke points in an attempted to win the new industrial century. Will it succeed? I'd just weigh multi thousand years civilizations that have survived empires and invasions vs. a 250 year old project called America. It's going to get a lot hotter.

🇺🇸⚔️🇮🇷🇷🇺 Before some rush to accuse Russia of not doing enough to help its allies in Iran, Venezuela and Syria here are a few things to consider:

First of all, Russia has absolutely zero mutual defense pacts or obligations to send troops to any of these states.  Their only treaties on that front are with North Korea and Belarus.  And in the case of Syria and Venezuela, they had Russian weaponry and the capabilities to defend themselves from US aggression but surrendered before it even began.  

Syrian troops were bribed by Qatar into laying down their arms and fleeing, while Bashar al-Assad abandoned his allies in Moscow and Tehran in favor of closer ties with the Gulf states who would back stab him when HTS launched the counteroffensive.  Delcy Rodriguez made a backroom deal with the CIA to give up Maduro and sell out the Venezuelan people to Western multinational corporations.

How and why is it Russia and China’s job to defend countries who won’t even fight for themselves?

text:
🚨 BlackRock Just Said No.

BlackRock’s $26 billion HPS Corporate Lending Fund received $1.2 billion in withdrawal requests in the first quarter — roughly 9.3% of its net asset value. The fund paid out $620 million before hitting the 5% quarterly cap and stopped honoring the rest.  Half the investors who wanted out were told to wait. The world’s largest asset manager — $11.5 trillion under management just gated its own clients.

BlackRock shares fell 5.7% on the NYSE  on the news. Shares of Apollo Global Management, Ares Management, and Blue Owl Capital all fell between 4% and 6% by midday. 

Earlier this week, Blackstone faced record withdrawal requests from its $82 billion private credit fund BCRED. The firm temporarily raised its redemption limit from 5% to around 7% and deployed roughly $400 million of its own capital, from the company and its employees — to meet all withdrawal requests.  BlackRock didn’t bother. It just said no.

The money always knows before the politicians admit it. BlackRock didn’t gate those investors because of a software portfolio and some bad auto loans — it gated them because the arteries of the global economy are being severed in real time, and the men who built their $3.5 trillion shadow banking empire on the assumption of stable supply chains and open sea lanes are now watching the Strait of Hormuz burn on their Bloomberg terminals while pretending it’s a liquidity management technicality. 

Gulf airports are grounded. Gulf oil infrastructure is burning after relentless Iranian bombardment.  Oil is up 31% in seven days while LNG is up by a catastrophic 650%. And somewhere in the bowels of a $26 billion fund, mid-market American companies that borrowed cheap to survive are about to discover what happens when the price of everything from fuel, freight, feedstock spikes simultaneously because Washington and Tel Aviv decided that international law was someone else’s problem. 

This isn’t a wake-up call. The alarm went off years ago. What you’re watching now is the building on fire, the exits locked, and the asset managers quietly telling half their clients hey sorry, you can’t leave yet. beware the law of natural consequences!

channel_title: Rerum Novarum // Intel, Breaking News, and Alerts 🇺🇸
text:
🇺🇸🇮🇷🇮🇱 - WAR IN IRAN | MARCH 12th, DAY 13 RECAP:

🇮🇷🇦🇪 - UKMTO reported that a Japanese container ship 35 nautical miles north of Jebel Ali, United Arab Emirates, was struck by a projectile, and a fire started on board. The crew was reported safe.

🇮🇷🇧🇭 - Iran struck Bahrain International Airport.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🇮🇱 - The U.S. and Israel continued strikes in Tehran and western Iran.

🇮🇷🇰🇼 - Damage was reported at Kuwait International Airport after an Iranian attack, according to AFP.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🇴🇲 - The U.S. Navy told the shipping industry that military escorts in the Strait of Hormuz were not possible for now, according to Reuters. The Pentagon and National Security Council reportedly “significantly underestimated Iran’s willingness to close the Strait of Hormuz,” according to CNN.

🛢 - The IEA estimated that Gulf countries reduced their oil production by at least 10 million barrels per day.

🇮🇷 - 3.2 million people were internally displaced in Iran, the United Nations Refugee Agency reported.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🇮🇱 - “We will obtain compensation from the enemy. If they refuse, we will take their assets to the extent we deem appropriate, and if that is not possible, we will destroy an equivalent amount of their property,” said Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran’s new Supreme Leader, in his first public statement.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🇮🇱 - “The Strait of Hormuz must remain closed,” said Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran’s new Supreme Leader, in his first public statement.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🇮🇱 - “Discussions were ongoing regarding the opening of other fronts in areas where the enemy has little experience and is highly vulnerable,” said Mojtaba Khamenei.

🇮🇹🇮🇶🇮🇷 - Overnight, an Italian military base in Erbil, Iraqi Kurdistan, was attacked by drones.

🇺🇸 - A washing machine malfunctioned on the USS Gerald R. Ford, causing a small fire and lightly injuring two U.S. servicemen in the main laundry room, according to CENTCOM. 

🇫🇷🛢 - TotalEnergies suspended 15% of its oil and gas production worldwide, effective immediately.

🇺🇸🇮🇱 - A mass shooting occurred at Temple Israel Synagogue in Bloomfield Hills, Michigan.

🛢 - Brent crude closed above $100 per barrel for the first time since 2022.

🇺🇸🇱🇧🇮🇱 - With Trump’s blessing, Netanyahu considered withdrawing from the historic Lebanon–Israel maritime border agreement.

🇫🇷🇮🇶 - The French base in Erbil, Iraqi Kurdistan, was struck by multiple suicide drones. One French soldier was killed and seven were wounded.

🇺🇸🇮🇶 - A U.S. KC-135 refueling aircraft crashed in western Iraq. Rescue operations were underway, and Iraqi groups claimed they struck the aircraft with an anti-aircraft missile.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🇸🇦 - 19 U.S. servicemembers were evacuated today from Saudi Arabia to Germany after being wounded in Iranian attacks, according to CBS News.

🇺🇸🇮🇷 - The U.S. fired on an Iranian vessel that sailed too close to the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier. The vessel was hit with two Hellfire missiles from a U.S. helicopter, and the status of the Iranian ship and crew was unknown, according to CBS News. The IRGC claims it was struck by drones and missiles.

🇺🇸🇮🇷 - The U.S. burned through “years” of critical munitions since the start of the war with Iran. Officials were especially worried about stocks of Tomahawk missiles, THAAD interceptors, and Patriot missiles. The military was expected to brief Congress on munitions expended in the coming days, according to the Financial Times.

Here's our recap from yesterday: https://x.com/officialrnintel/status/2031934772386189444?s=20

🇮🇷🇦🇪 - UKMTO reported that a Japanese container ship 35 nautical miles north of Jebel Ali, United Arab Emirates, was struck by a projectile, and a fire started on board. The crew was reported safe.

🇮🇷🇧🇭 - Iran struck Bahrain International Airport.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🇮🇱 - The U.S. and Israel continued strikes in Tehran and western Iran.

🇮🇷🇰🇼 - Damage was reported at Kuwait International Airport after an Iranian attack, according to AFP.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🇴🇲 - The U.S. Navy told the shipping industry that military escorts in the Strait of Hormuz were not possible for now, according to Reuters. The Pentagon and National Security Council reportedly “significantly underestimated Iran’s willingness to close the Strait of Hormuz,” according to CNN.

🛢 - The IEA estimated that Gulf countries reduced their oil production by at least 10 million barrels per day.

🇮🇷 - 3.2 million people were internally displaced in Iran, the United Nations Refugee Agency reported.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🇮🇱 - “We will obtain compensation from the enemy. If they refuse, we will take their assets to the extent we deem appropriate, and if that is not possible, we will destroy an equivalent amount of their property,” said Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran’s new Supreme Leader, in his first public statement.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🇮🇱 - “The Strait of Hormuz must remain closed,” said Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran’s new Supreme Leader, in his first public statement.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🇮🇱 - “Discussions were ongoing regarding the opening of other fronts in areas where the enemy has little experience and is highly vulnerable,” said Mojtaba Khamenei.

🇮🇹🇮🇶🇮🇷 - Overnight, an Italian military base in Erbil, Iraqi Kurdistan, was attacked by drones.

🇺🇸 - A washing machine malfunctioned on the USS Gerald R. Ford, causing a small fire and lightly injuring two U.S. servicemen in the main laundry room, according to CENTCOM. 

🇫🇷🛢 - TotalEnergies suspended 15% of its oil and gas production worldwide, effective immediately.

🇺🇸🇮🇱 - A mass shooting occurred at Temple Israel Synagogue in Bloomfield Hills, Michigan.

🛢 - Brent crude closed above $100 per barrel for the first time since 2022.

🇺🇸🇱🇧🇮🇱 - With Trump’s blessing, Netanyahu considered withdrawing from the historic Lebanon–Israel maritime border agreement.

🇫🇷🇮🇶 - The French base in Erbil, Iraqi Kurdistan, was struck by multiple suicide drones. One French soldier was killed and seven were wounded.

🇺🇸🇮🇶 - A U.S. KC-135 refueling aircraft crashed in western Iraq. Rescue operations were underway, and Iraqi groups claimed they struck the aircraft with an anti-aircraft missile.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🇸🇦 - 19 U.S. servicemembers were evacuated today from Saudi Arabia to Germany after being wounded in Iranian attacks, according to CBS News.

🇺🇸🇮🇷 - The U.S. fired on an Iranian vessel that sailed too close to the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier. The vessel was hit with two Hellfire missiles from a U.S. helicopter, and the status of the Iranian ship and crew was unknown, according to CBS News. The IRGC claims it was struck by drones and missiles.

🇺🇸🇮🇷 - The U.S. burned through “years” of critical munitions since the start of the war with Iran. Officials were especially worried about stocks of Tomahawk missiles, THAAD interceptors, and Patriot missiles. The military was expected to brief Congress on munitions expended in the coming days, according to the Financial Times.

text:
🇺🇸🇱🇧🇮🇱⚡- BREAKING: With Trump's blessing, Netanyahu is considering withdrawing from the historic Lebanon-Israel maritime border agreement.

The agreement was signed in 2022 after a decades-long dispute over the gas-rich fields of the Eastern Mediterranean. Dubbed 'Line 23', the previous agreement itself was a disappointment for Beirut, as it gave Israel full access to the Karish field, while sharing the Qana gas field with Tel Aviv.

Source: Israeli Channel 14.

channel_title: Rerum Novarum // Intel, Breaking News, and Alerts 🇺🇸
text:
🇺🇸🇮🇷🇮🇱 - WAR IN IRAN | MARCH 11th, DAY 12 RECAP:

🇮🇷🇴🇲🇦🇪 - UKMTO reports two vessels have been attacked with projectiles minutes apart. One vessel, 11 nautical miles off the coast of Oman in the Strait of Hormuz, has been struck, and the crew was evacuated. The second vessel was hit 50 nautical miles northwest of Dubai, United Arab Emirates.

🇦🇪🇮🇷 - Two Iranian drones made impact near Dubai International Airport, with four injuries reported.

🇮🇷🇨🇳 - Iran has shipped 11–12 million barrels of crude to China through the Strait of Hormuz since the start of the U.S. military operation in Iran, CNBC reports.

🇩🇪🛢 - Germany announced it will partially release its strategic oil reserves to offset the rise in oil prices.

🇯🇵🛢 - Japan has also announced it will take similar measures by releasing 15 days' worth of private-sector oil reserves and one month's worth of state oil reserves.

🛢 - The International Energy Agency has decided to release 400 million barrels from its strategic reserves.

🇪🇸🇮🇱 - Spain has reduced its relations with Israel, recalling its ambassador. Spain's embassy in Tel Aviv will instead be run by a Chargé d’Affaires.

🇨🇭🇺🇸🇮🇷 - Switzerland has closed its embassy in Tehran but is "maintaining an open communication line between Iran and the United States."

🇴🇲🇮🇷 - Oman has reported a direct drone hit on fuel tanks.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🇷🇺 - "Russia is giving Iran advice on using its one-way drones based on tactics Moscow learned from the war in Ukraine. The help includes evading air defenses and strategies for coordinating combined strikes," CNN reports, citing a Western intelligence source.

🇺🇸🇮🇷 - A preliminary U.S. military investigation found that a U.S. Tomahawk missile mistook an Iranian girls' school in Minab for an Iranian naval base.

🇺🇸🇮🇶🇮🇱 - U.S./Israeli airstrikes targeted the Iran-backed Popular Mobilization Forces in Mosul, Iraq.

🇺🇸🇦🇪🇮🇷 - Deloitte, PwC, and Citi are closing and evacuating their offices in Dubai following threats from Iran against U.S. firms in the Gulf.

🇮🇷 - Iran exports more oil through the Strait of Hormuz now than it did before the start of the U.S. military operation in the region, Kpler reports.

🇺🇸🇮🇷 - Iranian hackers claimed responsibility after a major cyberattack hit Stryker, a U.S. medical equipment company.

🇺🇸🇳🇴🇮🇶 - Three brothers of Iraqi origin have been arrested following the security incident at the U.S. Embassy in Oslo, Norway.

🇮🇷 - Iran has laid a dozen mines in the Strait of Hormuz, Reuters reports.

text:
🇺🇸🇮🇷🇮🇱 - WAR IN IRAN | MARCH 10th, DAY 11 RECAP:

🇺🇸🇮🇷⚡️- "Negotiations with the United States are no longer on the agenda." - Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi.

🇮🇷🇦🇪🇴🇲⚡️- A vessel was targeted north of Abu Dhabi, UAE.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🇮🇱⚡️- U.S. / Israel continue strikes; Israel targets oil depots.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🇮🇱⚡️- Iran's Red Crescent reports the deaths of 1,348 and more than 17,000 injured across Iran since the start of Operation Epic Fury.

🇦🇪🇮🇷⚡️- Abu Dhabi confirms an Iranian drone impacted the Ruwais Industrial Complex, the Middle East's largest refinery complex.

🇺🇸🏳️⚡️- The USA has designated the Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan as a "State Sponsor of Wrongful Detention."

🇨🇦🇺🇸⚡️- The U.S. consulate in downtown Toronto was targeted by gunfire early this morning.

🇺🇸🇮🇷⚡️- As many as 150 American troops have been wounded since the start of the Iran war, according to Reuters.

🇺🇸🇮🇷⚡️- U.S. intel has shown indications that Iran is preparing to deploy mines to the Strait of Hormuz. Iran is using smaller craft that can carry 2–3 mines each. - CBS News & CNN.

🇮🇷🇮🇱⚡️- Iran launches multiple waves of attacks on Israel, equaling or even surpassing the amount seen on the first day of the war.

🇱🇧🇮🇱⚡️- Hezbollah and the IDF continued to clash on the Lebanese-Israeli border.

🇮🇷🇸🇦🇦🇪⚡️- Iran launched attacks on over five different countries within a span of two hours or less.

🇺🇸🇮🇶⚡️- Two drones targeted the U.S. diplomatic facility near Baghdad International Airport; one drone directly struck the facility, sources tell WAPO and Reuters.

🇫🇷🛢- The International Energy Agency has proposed the largest release of oil reserves in history in order to bring down crude oil prices. The proposal will be decided on Wednesday. The release would exceed the 182 million barrels released in 2022 during Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. - WSJ.

Here's our recap from yesterday: https://x.com/officialrnintel/status/2031198202972536963?s=20

channel_title: Rerum Novarum // Intel, Breaking News, and Alerts 🇺🇸
text:
🇺🇸🇮🇷🇮🇱 - WAR IN IRAN | MARCH 9th, DAY 10 RECAP:

🇺🇸🇮🇶⚡️ - A drone strike targeted the U.S. base near Erbil Airport in Iraqi Kurdistan, according to Reuters.

🇮🇷🇧🇭⚡️ - Iran struck the BAPCO oil refinery in Bahrain; BAPCO declared force majeure (state of emergency) after it was hit.

🇺🇸🇺🇦🇯🇴⚡️ - Ukrainian President Zelensky says his country has sent drone specialists along with drone interceptors to Jordan to defend U.S. bases from Iranian one-way drones.

🇺🇸🇸🇾🇮🇷⚡️ - "Syrian Kurds warn Iranian Kurds against allying with the United States, citing their own experience when Washington 'abandoned' them after years of cooperation," - Reuters.

🇧🇪⚡️ - A synagogue in Liège, Belgium, was subject to an explosion at the entrance of the building; no casualties.

🇺🇸🇮🇱⚡️ - U.S. and Israel targeted central Iran multiple times within a few hours.

🇮🇷🇮🇱⚡️ - Multiple Iranian ballistic missile attacks, including cluster munitions, hit Israel; at least 2 confirmed deaths and multiple injuries.

🇺🇸🇮🇷⚡️ - The U.S. Navy struck three Iranian vessels in the Persian Gulf: two anchored off the coast of Bandar Lengeh, and the other at Bandar Kong.

🇸🇦⚡️ - Saudi Aramco has begun reducing crude output at two oilfields, Reuters reports.

🇺🇸🇹🇷⚡️ - The U.S. began evacuating its staff from the U.S. Consulate in Adana, southern Turkey.

🇱🇧🇮🇱⚡️ - Visual confirmation shows the IRGC successfully destroyed Israel’s SES satellite station in Beit Shemesh.

🇱🇧🇮🇱⚡️ - The IDF announced the death of one of its servicemen in southern Lebanon, a Staff Sergeant.

🇱🇧🇮🇱⚡️ - Hezbollah and the IDF clashed in Khiam, southern Lebanon; Hezbollah targeted and impacted at least two Israeli Merkava tanks.

🇫🇷🇮🇷⚡️ - “France is deploying about a dozen naval vessels, including its aircraft carrier strike group, to the Mediterranean, Red Sea, and potentially the Strait of Hormuz as part of defensive support to allies threatened by the conflict in the Middle East.” - Reuters.

🇺🇸🇮🇷⚡️ - “The U.S. has intercepted encrypted communications believed to have originated in Iran that may serve as 'an operational trigger' for 'sleeper assets' outside the country,” - ABC News.

🇦🇪🇮🇷⚡️ - A UAE military helicopter crashed while "on duty," presumably attempting to intercept Iranian projectiles; two crewmembers were killed.

🇺🇸🇮🇷⚡️ - The U.S. has now lost 11 MQ-9 drones in Iran, CBS reports.

🇺🇸🇮🇷⚡️ - "They made a big mistake. I don’t know if he's going to last," - President Trump on Mojtaba Khamenei becoming the new Supreme Leader.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🇮🇱⚡️ - "Any Arab or European nation that expels the ambassadors of Israel and the United States from its territory will, starting tomorrow, have full authority and freedom to pass through the Strait of Hormuz." - IRGC

🇺🇸🇮🇷⚡️ - "As we leave here, I could call it, or we could go further, and we're gonna go further." - President Trump.

🇺🇸🇮🇷⚡️ - President Trump says Iran has Tomahawk missiles and probably used them to bomb the Iranian girls’ school. (Iran has never owned Tomahawk cruise missiles.)

🇺🇸🇮🇷⚡️ - "We will determine the end of the war, not the American forces." - IRGC.

🇮🇶⚡️ - Drones impacted PJAK headquarters in Erbil, Iraqi Kurdistan.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🇧🇭⚡️ - Iranian drone strikes a high-rise building in Manama, Bahrain; the IRGC says 13 Americans were injured, and the Bahraini Interior Ministry confirms one death.

🇺🇸🇷🇺🇮🇷⚡️ - The President spoke with Russia's Putin over the phone to discuss the special military operation in Iran.

🛢⚡️ - Capital Economics, a research company cited in a WSJ article, estimates that Brent oil could reach $150 per barrel by mid-2026 in "the worst-case scenario."

🇭🇺🛢⚡️ - Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán said Hungary will limit fuel prices starting tomorrow due to the ongoing oil crisis.

🇺🇸🇮🇷🛢⚡️ - Oil futures plummet over 25% from their recent high, to $86/barrel. (It is noteworthy that oil futures are likely to skyrocket, since the war is continuing despite rhetoric.)

Here's our recap from yesterday: https://x.com/officialrnintel/status/2030844857237815316?s=20

🇺🇸🇷🇺🇮🇷⚡️ - The President spoke with Russia's Putin over the phone to discuss the special military operation in Iran.

text:
🇮🇷 In statement number 35 of Operation True Promise 4, Iran’s IRGC stated:

- A heavy barrage of "Kheibar Shekan" missiles with one-ton warheads against Zionist occupier targets marked the "forty-fourth" wave of Operation Promised Truth 4 with the blessed code "Ya Shadid al-'Iqab" on the night of the twenty-third of the holy month of Ramadan and on the eve of Quds Day, making it different for the hostile enemies.

- Areas of West Jerusalem, Tel Aviv, and Eilat in the occupied Palestinian territories were struck by Iranian missiles in this honorable operation, commemorating the memory of the martyrs of the Path of Jerusalem and power, especially the commander of the Promised Truths, Martyr Lieutenant General Hossein Salami.

- The "Muwaffaq Al-Salti" base and other U.S. bases in Manama and Erbil were targeted by the most severe missiles and drones of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.

- The air defense of the resistance front also succeeded in this operation in hitting a "Boeing KC-130 Stratotanker" refueling aircraft while it was refueling an aggressor fighter, killing 6 crew members.

- We will achieve the liberation of Al-Quds Al-Sharif and the destruction of the usurping Israel with divine help, and by trusting in Almighty God, we will open the doors of relief for the Islamic nation.

- American soldiers, leave the region immediately, or else we will bury you under the rubble wherever you are in the region, especially in hotels, tunnels, industrial towns, and underground shelters.

text:
🇺🇸⚔️🇮🇷 A Financial Times report says the Trump administration has already burned through years’ worth of key munitions since the war on Iran began, raising concerns about the Pentagon’s ability to replenish its stockpiles.

Sources told the paper that large numbers of long-range Tomahawk missiles have been used. One source described the spending as a “massive expenditure,” warning the US Navy will feel the impact for years.

The conflict has also disrupted a major maritime trade route and pushed oil prices above $100 per barrel. Rising fuel prices and fears of another prolonged Middle East war are fueling domestic opposition in the United States during a midterm election year.

The Pentagon is expected to request up to $50 billion in additional military funding from the White House and Congress, setting up a likely political battle in Washington over the cost of the war.

🔴 @DDGeopolitics | Socials | Donate | Advertising

🇺🇸⚔️🇮🇷 CBS reports that a U.S. Navy ship fired at an Iranian vessel that sailed close to the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier, citing two U.S. officials who spoke anonymously because they were not authorized to comment publicly.

According to the officials, the U.S. ship used a 5-inch Mark-45 naval gun but missed the Iranian vessel multiple times.

CBS also reports that a helicopter later launched two Hellfire missiles, striking the Iranian vessel.

U.S. Central Command declined to comment, telling CBS: “We have nothing for you on this.” The condition of the Iranian vessel and its crew is unknown.

ℹ️The article is vague about the Iranian vessel and does not specify whether it was military or civilian.

🐻 Update: This could also confirm the IRGC’s claim at the time that it targeted the aircraft carrier.

🇮🇷 Even Iranians opposed to the government do not support U.S. and Israeli strikes and are losing faith in regime change, the Financial Times reports after speaking with residents.

“We should not have been bombed. Our city, our country. This should never have happened. Why was there a clean, bloodless regime change in Venezuela, but not here?” said Mandana, whose apartment in central Tehran was hit during the strikes.

Civilian areas are also affected because attacks target police stations, military sites, and officials living in residential neighborhoods. According to official figures, more than 1,000 civilians have been killed and over 8,000 homes damaged or destroyed.

Nearly two weeks into the war, FT reports there are no signs of the anti-government protests that swept the country in January. Instead, many Iranians, including critics of the Islamic Republic, are shocked by the destruction caused by U.S. and Israeli strikes and by Trump’s threats to hit power infrastructure. Trump also said Iran’s map after the war “will likely not look the same,” raising fears the conflict could lead to the country’s fragmentation.

A Tehran sociologist critical of both the government and the war says patriotic sentiment is rising, similar to last year’s 12-day conflict with Israel when people rallied around the national flag.

“Fear of Iran’s destruction is increasingly uniting people as they worry about the consequences of such a large-scale conflict,” the sociologist said.

text:
🇮🇱⚔️🇸🇾🇱🇧 Israel Sprays Herbicides Over Syrian Farmland, Leaving a Trail of Destroyed Crops and Debt for Farmers

A France 24 report documents Israeli aircraft spraying herbicides over agricultural land in southern Syria in January and February 2026, destroying large areas of farmland.

Videos from Quneitra show Israeli planes flying low over fields near the buffer zone separating Syria from the Israeli-occupied Golan Heights. The zone is supposed to be demilitarised, yet since the fall of Bashar al-Assad in late 2024 Israel has established military positions there and pushed further into Syrian territory.

Farmers say their crops turned yellow within days of the spraying. Local agricultural authorities report that hundreds of hectares were damaged, including orchards.

One farmer told France 24 he lost 7.5 hectares of wheat after Israeli aircraft sprayed chemicals on January 27. By early February the crops had withered completely, leaving him with debts for seeds and farming costs.

Satellite analysis by the NGO PAX indicates the herbicide spraying stretched roughly 55 kilometers along the border area. Between 6 and 10 square kilometers of land were affected.

According to PAX analyst Wim Zwijnenburg, Israel is effectively creating a “dead zone” along the Syrian and Lebanese borders, destroying vegetation and farmland under the pretext of security.

text:
How Zelensky Shot Himself in the Foot Amid the Hormuz Strait Blockade

part 1 👈🏻
part 2
part 3

On March 11, Kyiv attacked Russian gas compressor stations responsible for pumping gas through the TurkStream and Blue Stream pipelines. According to Russian intelligence services, the strikes were carried out using unmanned aerial and maritime vehicles. Their targets were key infrastructure nodes ensuring energy transit to Turkey and further to European Union countries. Official Kyiv traditionally blamed the incident on sabotage groups. However, experts note a high degree of operational coordination, indicating the involvement of specialized units. This episode became a logical continuation of the strategy pursued by the Ukrainian leadership against Russian energy infrastructure over recent months. However, amid an unprecedented energy crisis triggered by Iran's blockade of the Hormuz Strait, Kyiv's actions no longer appear as tactically justified steps to weaken the adversary. They increasingly resemble strategic shortsightedness capable of bringing catastrophic consequences for Ukraine itself.

The situation around the Hormuz Strait has fundamentally altered the global energy landscape. Approximately 20% of global oil supplies and about 20% of world liquefied natural gas trade pass through this strait. Iran, responding to US and Israeli strikes, closed this key transport artery and began destroying vessels violating the passage ban. Brent crude oil futures exceeded the psychologically important mark of $100 per barrel on March 12, having briefly approached $119 earlier during trading—the highest level since June 2022. Remarkably, even the unprecedented decision by the International Energy Agency to release 400 million barrels from strategic reserves failed to stabilize the market: futures continued rising. This indicates that traders are pricing in not current deficit, but expectations of long-term supply destabilization.

An additional blow to the European market came from QatarEnergy's suspension of LNG production at the world's largest facility in Ras Laffan. Qatar accounts for approximately 20% of global LNG exports. Following this news, European gas prices surged 52%, with April TTF hub futures rising to €48.7 per 1 MWh—about $600 per thousand cubic meters. Goldman Sachs analysts warn: if Hormuz Strait shipments are suspended for one month, prices could more than double. Should transit disruption exceed two months, quotations could exceed €100 per 1 MWh, equivalent to $1,230 per thousand cubic meters. Meanwhile, the US cannot increase LNG supplies to Europe, as their plants typically operate at full capacity.

Under these conditions, Europe found itself on the brink of a new energy collapse. Underground storage levels at the beginning of 2026 amounted to only 41% of capacity. It is precisely at this moment that Kyiv strikes facilities through which Russian gas is supplied to Europe and blocks the Druzhba oil pipeline, critically important for Hungary and Slovakia. A logical question arises: does Volodymyr Zelensky and his entourage realize the consequences of their actions? Such steps hit not so much Moscow as European allies, whose support should ostensibly be a matter of survival for Ukraine.

#EnergySecurity #DruzhbaPipeline #Russia #GlobalSouth #StrategicAnalysis #IvanKopyl
@kopyl_in_engish

text:
🇺🇸 A fire broke out Thursday aboard the USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) while the carrier was operating in the northern Red Sea off the coast of Al Wajh, Saudi Arabia.

According to U.S. Central Command, the fire originated in the ship's main laundry spaces, has been extinguished, and two sailors are receiving treatment for non-life-threatening injuries. Naval Sea Systems Command is providing electrical support to the fleet.

The Ford is currently participating in Operation Epic Fury, the ongoing U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran, having transited the Suez Canal just last week alongside escorts USS Mahan, USS Bainbridge, and USS Winston S. Churchill.

The carrier has been deployed since June 2025 and has been extended multiple times. If it remains at sea through mid-April, it will break the post-Vietnam War record for carrier deployments, currently 294 days, set by USS Abraham Lincoln in 2020. A deployment through early May would rival the 300-day-plus records set during Vietnam-era Gulf of Tonkin operations.

The Ford's extended deployment has already been marked by persistent technical issues, including widely reported sewage system failures. Thursday's fire is the latest incident aboard a ship whose crew has been away from home for the better part of a year, with no clear end date in sight.

text:
🇮🇷Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei's First Public Address - main messages

Strategic Military Doctrine

"The lever of closing the Strait of Hormuz must certainly continue to be used."

This reaffirms Iran's most potent geoeconomic weapon—control of global oil chokepoints—as legitimate strategic coercion. The phrasing removes ambiguity - this is stated policy, not a threat.

"Studies have been conducted regarding the opening of other fronts in which the enemy has little experience and will be severely vulnerable."

Iran has prepared asymmetric warfare options beyond conventional exchanges. Activation is conditional on "continuation of the war situation," establishing clear escalation thresholds.

Retaliatory Framework

"We will take reparations from the enemy. If he refuses, we will take from his assets to the extent we deem appropriate. If that is not possible, we will destroy an equivalent amount of his assets."

Three-tiered framework codifies proportional response while reserving unilateral damage assessment authority—Iran alone determines equivalence.

Regional Posture and the Resistance Axis

"The matter of resistance and the resistance front is an inseparable part of the values of the Islamic Revolution."

This elevates the Resistance Front from tactical alliance to theological necessity, formalizing Yemen, Hezbollah, and Iraq as permanent strategic infrastructure.

"I recommend that the countries of the region close the American bases as soon as possible... because they must have understood by now that America's claim of establishing security and peace was nothing but a lie."

Attacks will target "only those bases," not host nations. This pressures regional governments to choose between US presence and Iranian retaliation.


🐻The address signals strategic continuity while introducing calibrated escalation protocols—Hormuz closure remains policy, new asymmetric fronts are prepared, and regional states face a clear ultimatum on US bases before they become unavoidable targets.

🇮🇷 The first message of Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Hosseini Khamenei, the Supreme Leader of the Islamic Revolution

Part One

In the name of God, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful

Mā nansakh min āyah aw nunsihā na'ti bikhayrin minhā aw mithlihā.

Peace be upon you, O caller to God and His divine signs, peace be upon you, O gate of God and judge of His religion, peace be upon you, O caliph of God and supporter of His truth, peace be upon you, O proof of God and guide of His will; peace be upon you, O anticipated leader; peace be upon you with all the comprehensive peace; peace be upon you, O master, owner of the time.

At the beginning of my speech, I must offer my condolences to my master, may God hasten his relief, on the occasion of the painful martyrdom of the great leader of the revolution, dear wise Khamenei, and I ask from him prayers of goodness for each and every one of the great nation of Iran and indeed all Muslims of the world, all servants of Islam and the revolution, the self-sacrificers, the survivors of the martyrs of the Islamic movement, especially the recent war, and for myself.

The second part of my speech is addressed to the great nation of Iran. Initially, I must briefly state my position regarding the vote of the respected Assembly of Experts. I, your servant Seyyed Mojtaba Hosseini Khamenei, learned of the result of the vote of the respected Assembly of Experts simultaneously with you and through the Islamic Republic's television. For me, sitting in a place that was the seat of two great leaders, the great Khomeini and the martyred Khamenei, is a difficult task. Because this seat has the history of someone who, after more than 60 years of striving in the way of God and foregoing all kinds of pleasures and comforts, became a shining gem and an outstanding figure not only in the present era but throughout the history of the rulers of this country. Both his life and the manner of his death were intertwined with glory and honor derived from reliance on the truth.

I had the honor to visit his body after his martyrdom; what I saw was a mountain of steadfastness, and I heard that his healthy fist was clenched. Those knowledgeable about the various aspects of his personality should speak at length. In this context, I suffice with this summary and leave the details to other appropriate occasions. This is the reason for the difficulty of sitting on the leadership seat after such a person; completing this gap is only possible with the help of God Almighty and your support, people.

Next, it is necessary to emphasize a point directly related to the essence of my speech. That point is that among the arts of the martyred leader and his great predecessor was involving the people in all arenas, continuously enlightening and informing them, and practically relying on their power. He thus actualized the true meaning of the public and publicness and was deeply committed to it. The clear effect of this was seen in these few days when the country was without a leader and without a commander-in-chief. The insight and wisdom of the great nation of Iran in the recent event, their steadfastness, courage, and presence, caused friends to admire and enemies to be astonished. It was you, the people, who led the country and guaranteed its strength. The verse cited at the beginning of this writing means that there is no verse of the divine verses whose time ends or is forgotten except that God Almighty replaces it with a better or similar one.

The reason for using this noble verse is not that I am on the level of the martyred leader, let alone to assume superiority over him; rather, it is to draw attention to the rightful and prominent role of you, dear nation. If that great blessing was taken from us, it was replaced by the renewed presence of the Iranian nation in this system. Know this: if your power does not appear on the scene, neither leadership nor any of the various institutions whose real role is to serve the people will have the necessary effectiveness.

text:
🇺🇸 The Trump administration quickly reversed its stance on releasing emergency oil reserves during the war on Iran.

Early Tuesday, U.S. officials told G7 partners that a large release was unnecessary after oil prices fell below $90 per barrel.

Less than two hours later, Trump changed course after advisers warned about market volatility and the risk of Iran closing the Strait of Hormuz.

The International Energy Agency then approved a record release of 400 million barrels from strategic reserves. The United States will supply more than 100 million barrels despite its own reserve being only about 60% full. Some allies were caught off guard by the sudden shift but agreed to support the move to prevent market instability.

text:
🇮🇶🇺🇸 Statement from the Popular Mobilization Forces Authority

The Popular Mobilization Forces Authority expresses its complete rejection and strong condemnation of the criminal air attacks that targeted its official headquarters in several Iraqi provinces. These attacks were carried out by U.S. aircraft in a blatant assault and a dangerous violation of Iraq’s sovereignty and a clear تجاوز against its official security institutions.

These attacks targeted PMF headquarters in the provinces of Diyala, Kirkuk, Anbar, Nineveh, Salah al-Din, Wasit, and Babil. These are official facilities operating within Iraq’s security system and in full coordination with the Joint Operations Command.

The total number of strikes reached 32 airstrikes. Since the beginning of this month, these attacks have resulted in the martyrdom of 27 fighters and the injury of 50 others from the PMF who were carrying out their national duty to defend Iraq, its security, and stability. Among them were 9 martyrs and 10 wounded in the air attacks that occurred today. We confirm that these headquarters had no role in targeting U.S. bases inside or outside Iraq, and that those who were martyred were innocent fighters performing their official duties. Some of them were stationed on the border to protect Iraq’s sovereignty and security.

The Popular Mobilization Forces Authority, as a fundamental part of Iraq’s state security system, affirms its full commitment to the directives of the Commander-in-Chief of the Armed Forces and operates in direct and continuous coordination with the Joint Operations Command in carrying out all its security and military duties.

In light of these serious attacks, the Popular Mobilization Forces Authority calls on all political forces to take a firm national stance and clear measures to stop these repeated attacks targeting our heroic security forces, which represent a direct violation of the country’s sovereignty and the dignity of its security forces.

The Authority also affirms that the blood of the martyrs will not be wasted, and that these attacks will not deter the sons of the Popular Mobilization Forces from continuing to perform their national and constitutional duty to protect Iraq and its people. The PMF will remain, as the Iraqi people know it, a strong fortress and a solid barrier against terrorism and anyone who seeks to undermine Iraq’s security, stability, and sovereignty.

Glory and eternity to our righteous martyrs
A speedy recovery to our wounded
Honor and sovereignty to Iraq and its people

Popular Mobilization Forces Authority
12 March 2026

text:
🇹🇷🇬🇷 Tensions are rising between Greece and Turkey over islands in the Aegean Sea.

After the attack on a British airbase in Cyprus, Greece deployed a Patriot air defense battery to the island of Karpathos in the eastern Aegean.

Turkey’s Defense Ministry says the move violates the island’s demilitarized status under the 1923 Treaty of Lausanne and the 1947 Paris Peace Treaty.

Ankara stated that militarizing the islands does not change their legal status and accused Greece of exploiting regional crises. Turkey said it rejects Athens’ actions and will take all necessary measures.

At the same time, Turkey recently deployed fighter jets to the northern part of Cyprus under its control.

🇦🇪 UAE Ministry of Defense statement:

UAE air defenses intercepted 6 ballistic missiles, 7 cruise missiles, and 39 drones.

On March 11, 2026, UAE air defenses dealt with 6 ballistic missiles, 7 cruise missiles, and 39 drones launched from Iran.

Since the beginning of the Iranian attack, UAE air defenses have intercepted 268 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, and 1,514 drones.

These attacks have resulted in 6 deaths among Emirati, Pakistani, Nepali, and Bangladeshi nationals, and 131 minor to moderate injuries among nationals of the UAE, Egypt, Sudan, Ethiopia, the Philippines, Pakistan, Iran, India, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Azerbaijan, Yemen, Uganda, Eritrea, Lebanon, Afghanistan, Bahrain, the Comoros, Turkey, Iraq, Nepal, Nigeria, Oman, Jordan, Palestine, Ghana, and Indonesia.

The Ministry of Defense confirmed it remains on full readiness to respond to any threats and will firmly confront anything that seeks to undermine the country’s security, ensuring the protection of its sovereignty, security, stability, national interests, and capabilities.

text:
🇮🇷 SHAHED-136 | Loitering Munition / Kamikaze Drone

شاهد ۱۳۶ | “Witness-136”

The Shahed-136 forced militaries to rethink the economics of air defense. Not because it is technologically advanced, but because it is cheap, simple, and produced in large numbers.

Key specifications:

🔸️Range: ~2,500 km
🔸️Warhead: ~50 kg high explosive
🔸️Type: Loitering munition that autonomously navigates to the target area and dives on impact
🔸️Propulsion: Small piston engine with rear pusher propeller
🔸️Cost: ~$20,000–50,000 per unit
🔸️Guidance: INS/GPS with reported Kometa-M anti-jam receiver

The cost imbalance is what makes the system effective. An AIM-120 AMRAAM interceptor costs roughly $1–2 million, while a Patriot PAC-3 interceptor can reach $4 million. A Shahed-136 costs tens of thousands. Intercepting it with high-end air defense missiles creates a heavily unfavorable exchange rate for the defender.

Swarm tactics amplify this effect, forcing air defenses to expend large numbers of expensive interceptors.

Limitations:

The drone is subsonic, propeller-driven, and relatively loud. It can be destroyed by autocannon, short-range missiles, and even small arms at close range. Electronic warfare can also disrupt its GPS guidance. The 50 kg warhead limits effectiveness against hardened military targets.

Operational use:

Russia began deploying the system in large numbers in 2022 against Ukrainian energy infrastructure, using the designation Geran-2.

Iran also used Shahed-136 drones in the April 2024 strike on Israel as part of a layered attack that combined drones, cruise missiles, and ballistic missiles to saturate air defenses.

text:
🇮🇷 PAVEH | Cruise Missile

پاوه | Named after a city in Kermanshah Province

Paveh is a border city in Kermanshah province that saw heavy fighting on the western front of the Iran-Iraq War. Iraqi forces occupied the area in 1979 before the formal start of the war, and the region remained contested for years. Like Soumar, the name reflects the legacy of that conflict. Iran’s cruise missile program repeatedly draws on locations tied to the war.

Paveh is the direct successor to the Soumar cruise missile, built on the same technological lineage but with a key upgrade: extremely low flight altitude.

Key specifications:

🔸️Range: ~1,650 km
🔸️Warhead: 410–450 kg high explosive
🔸️Propulsion: Turbojet
🔸️Flight altitude: Less than 50 meters above ground level
🔸️Guidance: Improved INS/TERCOM navigation system

Most ground-based radar systems have a minimum detection altitude. Below that threshold, targets blend into ground clutter. Paveh is designed to fly beneath this level for most of its flight. At roughly 50 meters above terrain, following hills and valleys, it can remain undetected by conventional radar until it approaches its target.

The upgraded guidance package improves navigation accuracy during long-distance, low-altitude flight, addressing the drift that can accumulate over extended terrain-hugging routes.

Operational use:

Paveh is Iran’s newest long-range cruise missile, unveiled in 2023. It was reportedly used in April 2024 during Iran’s direct strike on Israel, launched as part of a combined salvo of ballistic missiles, cruise missiles, and drones intended to saturate Israeli air defenses.

text:
SOUMAR | Cruise Missile

سومار | Named after a border town from the Iran-Iraq War

Soumar is a small Kurdish-Iranian border town in Kermanshah province that became a frontline battleground during the Iran-Iraq War, changing hands multiple times between 1980 and 1988.

Like Dezful and Khorramshahr, the name reflects Iran’s practice of turning the memory of that war into symbols of future deterrence.

The missile itself is Iran’s reverse-engineered version of the Soviet Kh-55, originally an air-launched cruise missile.

Key specifications:

🔸️Range: ~2,500 km
🔸️Status: Iran’s longest-range cruise missile
🔸️Warhead: 400–450 kg high explosive
🔸️Propulsion: Turbojet
🔸️Flight profile: Low-altitude terrain-hugging flight
Guidance: INS with TERCOM (Terrain Contour Matching)

TERCOM guidance gives Soumar its strategic value. Instead of relying only on GPS, which can be jammed or spoofed, the missile compares radar altimeter readings with preloaded terrain maps to correct its flight path. Flying low for most of its 2,500 km range allows it to remain below radar coverage for much of the journey.

Limitations:

Cruise missiles are slower than ballistic missiles. Soumar’s subsonic speed means longer exposure time during flight. At maximum range, the missile may spend hours in transit.

Operational status:

There is no confirmed combat use in a major strike so far. However, with a range of 2,500 km, Soumar can reach targets across the Middle East, southeastern Europe, and North Africa from launch points inside Iran.

text:
🇮🇷 SEJJIL | Intermediate-Range Ballistic Missile

سجیل | “Stones of Baked Clay”

The name comes from Surah Al-Fil (Chapter of the Elephant) in the Quran. It refers to the stones dropped by birds sent by God to destroy the army of Abraha, the Yemeni ruler who marched war elephants toward Mecca to demolish the Kaaba. The army was destroyed before reaching its target.

Iran named its longest-range solid-fuel ballistic missile after those stones. The message is clear: punishment for those who march against what is considered sacred.

The missile itself marks a major step in Iran’s IRBM program. Sejjil is Iran’s first two-stage solid-fuel ballistic missile built entirely with domestic technology. It demonstrates how far Iran’s missile program has advanced.

Key specifications:

🔸️Range: 2,000–2,500 km (covers southern Europe and all U.S. bases in the region)
🔸️Warhead: 500+ kg high explosive
🔸️Fuel: Solid fuel, two-stage
🔸️Speed: Mach 13+ in terminal phase
🔸️Launch: Road-mobile, rapid deployment

Terminal velocity combined with solid fuel and road mobility makes interception extremely difficult. There is no fueling window. The launcher deploys and fires. From launch detection to impact at 2,000 km, response time is measured in minutes, while the terminal interception window shrinks to seconds.

Development history:

First tested in 2008, with additional tests through 2011. After that, Iran largely stopped public demonstrations of the system, widely interpreted as deliberate concealment while the program matured.

Sejjil is now confirmed to be part of Iran’s operational missile inventory. With a range of up to 2,500 km and hypersonic terminal velocity, it represents the upper limit of Iran’s current solid-fuel strike capability and its most survivable long-range missile platform.

🔴 @DDGeopolitics | Socials | Donate | Advertising

On March 11, U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) is issuing a warning to civilians that the Iranian regime is using civilian ports along the Strait of Hormuz to conduct military operations that threaten international shipping.

text:
🛢️📈Oil prices rise to $90 a barrel despite G7 statement.

Oil prices are trading higher again, returning to $90 a barrel, as markets doubted whether the International Energy Agency’s reported plan for a record release of oil reserves could offset potential supply shocks from the Iran war.

Brent crude has see-sawed this morning, dipping earlier but has now risen 2.5% to $90.05 a barrel.

This is despite energy ministers from the G7 group of nations releasing a statement after a meeting with the IEA on Tuesday that they support, in principle, proactive measures to address oil supply and market volatility, including the use of strategic oil reserves. They also plan on engaging with non member countries like China and India to open up on the strategic oil reserves.

text:
🇪🇺 Discontent with Ursula von der Leyen is growing inside the European Parliament and could threaten her position, Politico reports citing sources.

Socialist lawmakers from the ruling coalition plan to sharply question and criticize the European Commission chief today over her recent remark that “Europe can no longer be the guardian of the old world order, a world that is gone and will not return.”

Some interpreted the statement as support for Donald Trump’s policies.

“I hope President von der Leyen will clarify these statements, which surprised and angered both me and my group,” said Iratxe García, leader of the center-left Socialists and Democrats. “Questioning the need to defend the international order is something we cannot accept.”

Spanish S&D MEP Jonás Fernández said von der Leyen is “practically asking for a vote of no confidence.”

Three diplomats told Politico that the European Parliament is quickly becoming the biggest threat to von der Leyen finishing her term.

text:
🇷🇺 Sysert is a small town about 40 km south of Yekaterinburg. It was founded in 1732 as a workers’ settlement around an ironworks — one of many industrial towns that appeared in the Urals during the Russian Empire’s push to develop the region’s metallurgy. Sysert officially became a city only in 1946, when wartime industry — especially the production of hydraulic turbines — and new railway connections expanded the settlement.

The town is also known as the birthplace of Pavel Bazhov, the author of the famous Ural fairy tales. His childhood home still exists today — a simple peasant estate that shows what everyday life in a provincial Ural settlement looked like in the late 19th century.

text:
🇺🇸⚔️🇮🇷 US Senator Chris Murphy:

I was in a 2 hour briefing today on the Iran War. All the briefings are closed, because Trump can't defend this war in public.

I obviously can't disclose classified info, but you deserve to know how incoherent and incomplete these war plans are.

Here's what I can share:

Maybe the lead is that the war goals DO NOT involve destroying Iran's nuclear weapons program. This is, uh...surprising...since Trump says over and over this is a key goal.

But then of course we already know air strikes can't wipe out their nuclear material.

Second, they confirmed "regime change" is also NOT on the list. So, they are going to spend hundreds of billions of your taxpayer dollars, get a whole bunch of Americans killed, and a hardline regime - probably a MORE anti-American hardline regime - will still be in charge.

Ok, so what ARE the goals? It seems, primarily, destroying lots of missiles and boats and drone factories. 

But the question that stumped them: what happens when you stop bombing and they restart production?

They hinted at more bombing. Which is, of course, endless war.

And on the Strait of Hormuz, they had NO PLAN. I can't go into more detail about how Iran gums up the Strait, but suffice it say, right now, they don't know how to get it safely back open.

Which is unforgiveable, because this part of the disaster was 100% foreseeable.

— 🇮🇷/🇺🇸/🇮🇱 Key Points From Iran’s New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei’s Speech:

Iran’s new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei delivered his first address since assuming leadership.

Key points related to the ongoing war:

• Iran will continue using the closure of the Strait of Hormuz as leverage against its enemies.
• Iran is studying the opening of additional fronts where the enemy is weak if the war continues.
• Khamenei praised the Resistance Front, including Yemen, Hezbollah, and Iraqi groups, for supporting Iran.
• Iran says it targeted foreign bases used in the recent attack, warning it will continue doing so if they are used again.
• Tehran says retaliation for those killed in the war will continue, and revenge for the martyrs remains open.
• Khamenei called for unity among the Iranian people and continued support for the country’s fighters.

text:
⚡️🇶🇦🇮🇷🇬🇧 Mr. Vegas here, I've got one more story for you that we got second-hand:

An Al Jazeera presenter hosted a British academic to discuss the current political situation in the ongoing war. Naturally, the academic kept accusing the US of violating international law by attacking Iran.  

Out of the blue, the presenter interrupted her and said: 
"Since you're talking about international law in relation to what happened with Iran, what about what Iran has done to neighboring countries in the region? They've targeted several Gulf states. As for the bases, they're our bases and we're free to do with them as we please." (???) 

The academic replied that Iran had indeed been attacked and claimed that Iran was targeting US interests and bases in the region, and that the ones threatening regional stability are the US and Israel.  

The presenter had an issue with what she said and interrupted again, saying: 
"How does international law justify Iran targeting friendly and neighboring countries? This is ridiculous!"  

The British academic simply replied: 
"That's a good point. I think it's [also] important for Gulf countries to close down US military bases on their territories..."  

The presenter then cut off the call on live TV before the academic could even finish her sentence.

And you thought they had journalistic dignity.

text:
⚡️🇺🇸 One Last Hurrah

The USS Nimitz, slated to decommission later this year in 2026 will be deployed one final time.

The Nimitz is being deployed for her final deployment to the Southern Command, to the Southern Atlantic where the USS Gerald R. Ford has recently left. According to a representative of the US Navy, the Nimitz will be making multiple port calls in Latin America while enroute to the Southern Atlantic before ending her campaign in her new homeport, Norfolk Naval Yard where she will undergo formal decommissioning procedures. 

The USS Nimitz is the oldest aircraft carrier in service with the US, commissioned in 1975, named for Fleet Commander Chester Nimitz of World War 2. The Nimitz’s first major piece of action was the Iranian Hostage Crisis in 1979 after filming the movie ‘The Final Countdown’. The Nimitz was initially slated to undergo decommissioning in Q1 of 2026, however due to current geopolitical situations have opted the Department of the Navy to postpone it to Late April / Early May at the current state of affairs.

Once the Nimitz is fully decommissioned and slated for scrapping; the US will be left with 10 instead of 11 carriers until 2029; nearly 3 full years, which will cause more stress on the current fleet at the current rate of usage.

@Medmannews

text:
— 🇮🇷 BREAKING: Mojtaba Khamenei Appointed Supreme Leader Of The Islamic Republic Of Iran

Iran’s Fars News Agency reports that Mojtaba Khamenei has been appointed as the new Supreme Leader of Iran following the death of his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

Full statement below:
In the name of God, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful

Greetings and God's blessings be upon the noble and free people of Islamic Iran.

The Assembly of Experts of the Leadership, while expressing condolences on the martyrdom of the great leader His Eminence Ayatollah Imam Khamenei (may God sanctify his pure soul) and other esteemed martyrs, especially the honorable commanders and the devoted armed forces and the students of Shajareh Tayyebeh School in Minab County, and condemning the brutal aggression of the criminal America and the wicked Zionist regime, hereby informs that this Assembly, immediately after the news of the martyrdom and heavenly ascension of the wise and prudent leader of the Islamic Revolution, despite the severe wartime conditions and direct threats from enemies against this popular institution and the bombing of the offices of the Secretariat of the Assembly of Experts of the Leadership, which led to the martyrdom of several staff members and the security team of this body, did not hesitate for a moment in the process of selecting and introducing the leadership of the Islamic system. According to the duties stipulated in the Constitution and the internal regulations of the Assembly of Experts, it took the necessary measures and arrangements to hold an extraordinary session and introduce the new leader, and appropriate planning and coordination were carried out for the gathering of the honorable representatives of this Assembly who are present in all parts of the country, so that despite the intelligent provisions in Article 111 of the Constitution for the formation of a temporary council, the country would not face a leadership vacuum.

The Assembly of Experts of the Leadership, valuing the high position of the Guardianship of the Jurist in the era of the occultation of the Imam Mahdi (may God hasten his reappearance) and the importance of the leadership issue in the Islamic Republic system, honors 47 years of wise governance based on the principle of dignity, independence, and authority of the Imams of the Revolution, and while commemorating the memory of those divine and popular leaders, declares that after thorough and extensive review and utilizing the capacity of Article 108 of the Constitution, in accordance with its religious duty and belief in being in the presence of Almighty God, in today's extraordinary session, Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Hosseini Khamenei (may God preserve him) is appointed and introduced as the third leader of the sacred system of the Islamic Republic of Iran based on the decisive vote of the honorable representatives of the Assembly of Experts of the Leadership.

In conclusion, with appreciation for the members of the temporary council of Article 111 of the Constitution, it invites all the noble people of Iran, especially the elites and intellectuals of the seminary and university, to pledge allegiance to the leadership and maintain unity around the axis of the Guardianship, and requests the continued grace and favor of the Almighty God upon this country and its great people.

Peace, mercy, and blessings of God be upon you
Assembly of Experts of the Leadership
1404/12/17 (Iranian calendar)

@interbellumnews

text:
🇮🇷⚡️🇺🇸 I have been meaning to say this for a very long time. Many people say they have read Clausewitz but rarely do I see anyone bringing him up for discourse. I spent a huge portion of my life studying his work as an amateur, (Still halfway through) but I will try my best to explain.

War often exposes whether leaders actually understand the mechanics of war, or whether they have started believing their own propaganda.

The Prussian Strategist Carl von Clausewitz approached war almost like a science. Forces, resistance, friction, momentum. His core idea was simple: the objective of war is to break the enemy’s ability to resist, not simply to cause destruction.

Armies, command structures, logistics hubs, supply depots, airbases, radar networks, and military production facilities are the things that actually sustain a war effort. Destroy those, and the opponent’s capacity to continue fighting begins to collapse.

By contrast, bombing civilian areas rarely achieves that goal.

Civilian bombing can include two very different categories.

1: Military-relevant industry. Factories producing weapons, ammunition, vehicles, or other materials directly feeding the war effort. These can be legitimate strategic targets because they support the military system.

2: Non-military civilian targets: Residential neighborhoods, ordinary infrastructure, and population centers that have little direct impact on the enemy’s fighting capability.

The second category is where strategy often begins to break down.

Clausewitz would say the reason is structural. A war is fought by a system: military forces, leadership, logistics, industry, and public will. If the military arm of that system remains intact, the system keeps functioning. You can terrorize the population, but as long as the army still exists and the leadership still commands it, resistance continues.

The psychological piece matters too. Clausewitz described what he called the remarkable trinity of war: people, military, and government. If an attack accidentally unifies those three, the attacker has just made the defender stronger. That is the opposite of the intended effect.

There is also a political illusion involved. Leaders sometimes believe civilian suffering will force a population to overthrow its own government. History shows this rarely works. Societies under attack usually blame the attacker first. National identity hardens instead of collapsing.

Civilian punishment strategies often appear when the attacker cannot find or destroy the military targets they actually need. Intelligence gaps, hardened infrastructure, dispersed forces, these make the decisive targets difficult to reach. Leaders then drift toward striking what they can reach rather than what they should reach.

Strategy quietly mutates into "spectacle". (We see this in today's wars all the time)

Civilian populations endured massive bombing during the The Blitz and continued fighting. Entire cities across continents were devastated during World War II, yet resistance continued until military defeat occurred. In conflicts such as the Vietnam War, enormous firepower was used against civilian areas without breaking the opposing military system. During the Soviet-Afghan War, widespread destruction did not collapse the resistance either. The lesson has remained consistent across centuries.

Wars are not decided by spectacle. They are decided by systems. Armies, logistics, leadership, and industrial capacity. As long as those systems survive, the ability to fight survives.

War is not about how much destruction can be inflicted.
It is about whether the enemy still retains the capacity and the will to fight.

That is exactly why Clausewitz remains relevant centuries later.

PS: Nihilists can go fuck themselves.

@medmannews

text:
⚡⚡🇨🇳
Regarding 🇯🇵 Japan:
Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi:
 
The direction of Sino-Japanese relations is a choice Japan must make.

Last year marked the 80th anniversary of the victory of the Chinese People's War of Resistance Against Japanese Aggression. In this special year, Japan must deeply reflect on its mistaken path, including its atrocities of invading and colonizing Taiwan. However, Japan's current leadership has asserted that a Taiwan crisis would constitute a "situation threatening the survival of Japan" and that, based on this, it could exercise the right of collective self-defense. As is well known, the exercise of the right of self-defense is premised on an armed attack on the home country. I would like to ask: Taiwan affairs are China's internal affairs, so what right does Japan have to intervene? If an incident were to occur in the Taiwan region of China, what right does Japan have to exercise the right of self-defense? Does the so-called right of collective self-defense mean that the Peace Constitution, which renounces the right to wage war, would be null and void?  When we recall the frequent instances in the past when Japanese militarism launched foreign aggression under the pretext of a "life-threatening situation," the Chinese people and those throughout Asia cannot help but feel deep vigilance and concern, wondering, "Where exactly is Japan heading?" 

This year marks another 80th anniversary—the 80th anniversary of the Tokyo Trials. Eighty years ago, judges from 11 countries conducted court hearings over two and a half years, presenting a vast amount of irrefutable evidence that clearly demonstrated the repeated crimes of Japanese militarism. Today, 80 years later, history has once again presented Japan with an opportunity to examine its conscience and reflect. I hope that the Japanese people will remain vigilant and not allow anyone to overlook their own limitations and repeat the same mistakes. China, now a powerful and prosperous country, and its 1.4 billion people, will never tolerate anyone attempting to undo our history of colonization and aggression.

⚡⚡🇨🇳 During today's session the Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi has met the press to announce China's foreign policy and relations with the world.

Taiwan:

FM Wang Yi: Taiwan has been an integral part of China since ancient times. It never was, is not, and never will be a country. Its return to China is a victorious outcome of the Chinese People’s War of Resistance Against Japanese Aggression and the Second World War. 

Its status has been definitively fixed by a series of international legal instruments, including the Cairo Declaration, the Potsdam Proclamation, Japan’s Instrument of Surrender, and Resolution 2758 of the U.N. General Assembly. Any attempt to create “two Chinas” or “one China, one Taiwan” is doomed to fail.

Resolving the Taiwan question and realizing the complete reunification of our motherland is a historic process that cannot be stopped. Those who support it are on the right side of history; those who defy it shall perish.

text:
— 🇮🇷/🇺🇸 NEW: Iranian FM Says U.S. Strike Hit Freshwater Facility On Qeshm Island

Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said the United States carried out a strike on a freshwater desalination facility on Qeshm Island, which he said disrupted water supplies to around 30 villages.

Araghchi described the attack as a “blatant and desperate crime” and warned that targeting Iranian infrastructure represents a dangerous escalation with serious consequences.

Desalination plants are critical infrastructure across the Gulf, where many countries depend on them for a large share of their drinking water supply. Analysts have long warned that attacks on such facilities could have major humanitarian consequences if they were targeted during a regional conflict.

text:
⚡⚡🇮🇱🇮🇷🇺🇸 
Brajmohan Singh, a journalist from Indian Sadhana News, is describing his experience after returning from Israel:

When a missile strikes, you are not distinguished as Indian or Israeli (Indians face racism there). Only the bunker can protect you. I have seen people die in a 100-foot deep bunker, and they (the Israelis) do not give you accurate information. The freedom of the press in India is much better compared to Israel. We are grateful to the Indian government; they provide detailed information. In Israel, the government tells you nothing. You cannot take pictures of dead bodies, and you cannot visit hospitals. When an incident occurs, we are not given details of our location. The next day, when we visit the site, we are told, "Only one person was injured," but a local tells us, "There were four houses, and everyone died," which shows that a major incident happened.

﻿
(This Indian journalist was in Israel from February 28 to March 6.)

text:
Расчет РУК ZALA «Ланцет» сорвал ротацию ВСУ в Запорожье

В ходе воздушной разведки в ночное время оператор БПЛА ZALA Z-16 обнаружил движение бронетехники противника по полевым дорогам. Первую машину уничтожили во время движения точным ударом барражирующего боеприпаса «Ланцет». 

«Вторая бронемашина успела высадить группу противника в районе лесополосы, после чего также была поражена… Попытка подвоза резервов к переднему краю была сорвана, а использование маршрута движения техники на данном участке нарушено», – сообщили в Минобороны России. 

@ZalaAero
text_translated:
Calculation of RUK ZALA "Lancet" disrupted the rotation of the Armed Forces of Ukraine in Zaporozhye

During aerial reconnaissance at night, the operator of the ZALA Z-16 UAV discovered the movement of enemy armored vehicles along field roads. The first vehicle was destroyed while moving by a precise strike from a Lancet loitering munition. 

“The second armored vehicle managed to land an enemy group in the area of ​​the forest belt, after which it was also hit... An attempt to transport reserves to the front line was thwarted, and the use of the vehicle’s route in this area was disrupted,” the Russian Ministry of Defense reported. 

@ZalaAero

text:
⠀
🔴  Проект МАКСИМА ГРИГОРЬЕВА "Люди доброй воли" — интервью на фронте с добровольцами - участниками СВО.

Смотреть на Rutube

Смотреть предыдущее интервью

Доброволец с позывным "Кучер", 95-й стрелковый полк:

00:00 "За два с половиной года службы запомнился каждый день. Это война, и забыть что-то здесь сложно.

00:45 У меня есть награда за разминирование. Мы помогали во Владимировке — разминировали поле, чтобы наши ребята могли зайти в "зелёнку". Есть награды за участие в специальной операции и за отличие в военной службе.

01:35 Я родился в Донецкой области в 1994 году, вырос уже при Украине, учился в украинской школе. Тогда мне казалось, что русский и украинский это один язык. Сейчас понимаю, что это не только разные языки — это ещё и разный менталитет, разные взгляды на жизнь и на людей.

02:23 Очень плохо я отношусь к противнику. Мы через всё это прошли с 2014 года, через постоянные обстрелы. Они на нас напали, и мы должны давать отпор.

02:45 Со мной служат разные люди. В нашем расчёте один боец из Горловки, тоже из Донецкой области, один из Степановки — это село под Ростовом. Есть и человек из Сербии, который приехал добровольцем. Все хорошие люди.

03:10 Тем, кто думает стать добровольцем, я бы пожелал смелости и решимости. Нужно приходить и биться до конца. Если все будут думать, что это не их война и кто-то другой всё сделает, такую войну не выиграть. Это наш дом, наша семья, наша земля, и её нужно освобождать от этой нечисти".

#Людидобройволи #Донбасс #СВО #преступлениякиевскогорежима #БезСрокаДавности
